title: A finite convex combination stays inside a uniform absolute-value bound

source.notes: Generated from checked Lean ground truth; source repository withheld during public release.

informal.statement: A finite weighted sum with nonnegative weights summing to one remains bounded by M whenever every input is bounded by M in absolute value.
informal.latex: a_i\ge 0,\ \sum_{i\in s} a_i=1,\ |x_i|\le M \Rightarrow |\sum_{i\in s} a_i x_i|\le M.
assumptions: s is a finite index set
the weights sum to one over s
x is uniformly bounded by M on s
informal.expected_conclusion: The absolute value of the finite weighted sum is at most M.

declarations.name: finiteWeightedAbsSum_le
declarations.statement: theorem finiteWeightedAbsSum_le {ι : Type} (s : Finset ι) (a x : ι → Real) (M : Real) (ha : ∀ i, i ∈ s → 0 <= a i) (hsum : ∑ i ∈ s, a i = 1) (hx : ∀ i, i ∈ s → |x i| <= M) : |∑ i ∈ s, a i * x i| <= M

tasks.input: Translate the finite convex-combination absolute-value bound into Lean 4.
tasks.target: theorem finiteWeightedAbsSum_le {ι : Type} (s : Finset ι) (a x : ι → Real) (M : Real) (ha : ∀ i, i ∈ s → 0 <= a i) (hsum : ∑ i ∈ s, a i = 1) (hx : ∀ i, i ∈ s → |x i| <= M) : |∑ i ∈ s, a i * x i| <= M

tasks.input: Complete the Lean proof using the finite triangle inequality, nonnegative weights, and the row-sum identity.
tasks.target: Use Finset.abs_sum_le_sum_abs, abs_mul, abs_of_nonneg, Finset.sum_le_sum, Finset.sum_mul, and the weight-sum identity.

tasks.input: Find the finite-sum absolute-value inequality needed for the proof.
